Aperture Essays Archive Subscribe

Field Notes · Issue 14

The long, slow craft of making things that last.

For a decade we have published one essay a month — no more, no less. This is the record of what we learned holding that line.

Restraint as a feature

The first instinct of every new contributor is to ship more: more words, more sections, more proof. The discipline that took us years to learn was the opposite — to cut until only the load-bearing idea remained.

A page that asks the reader to do less work earns the right to ask for their full attention. That is the whole trade. Everything in our house style descends from it.

We measure an essay not by its length but by how little of it a reader could remove without the argument falling over.

In this issue

On typography as argument.

The economics of one-a-month.

Why we never added comments.

A reading list for slow weeks.
